qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Low-privilege formula validation allows Twig-based worker exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets FormulasController::actionValidateFormula and Formulas::evaluateFormula with a missing Commerce permission gate and expensive Twig rendering, while the advisory fixes stored XSS in InventoryController::actionInventoryLevelsTableData by HTML-encoding inventory descriptions and SKUs before rendering.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No in-scope final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's stored XSS in the Commerce inventory rendering path. The only materialized final finding is a low-privilege formulas-validation DoS on a different controller, entrypoint, and root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes stored XSS on the Commerce inventory page where Product Title, Variant Title, and Variant SKU are rendered without proper HTML escaping, leading to JavaScript execution when /admin/commerce/inventory is viewed.

The upstream fix commit 9f0638a4fb29ed8295a463385a7cc49ec986e33a changes src/controllers/InventoryController.php in actionInventoryLevelsTableData() and adds Html::encode() around the purchasable description, fallback description, and SKU before inventory-page output.

The only in-scope final qualifying finding, formula_validation_dos, instead reports that src/controllers/FormulasController.php exposes POST /actions/commerce/formulas/validate-formula without the BaseCpController permission check and that src/services/Formulas.php renders attacker-controlled Twig formulas, causing worker exhaustion rather than stored XSS.
